response.justification: Bogdan is a versatile, experienced full-stack engineer with 10+ years across web, backend, embedded, and high-throughput systems, plus a CS degree and relevant languages (C/C++, Java, JS/TS, C#). However, the role is a Principal/IC6 position specifically centered on growth and experimentation systems—A/B testing frameworks, feature flagging, telemetry/metrics infrastructure, and statistical analysis—and the resume shows essentially no direct experience in that domain. His seniority trajectory (Senior/Staff at mostly smaller companies) and lack of demonstrated experimentation-platform or large-scale measurement work make him a stretch for Principal-level scope. Worth a screening conversation only if the team is open to a strong generalist who would ramp into the experimentation domain.
strengths: Broad, deep engineering experience with strong fundamentals and relevant languages including C/C++ and Java
Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work (microservices handling millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
Strong software-craft practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and teaching/mentoring track record
concerns: No demonstrated experimentation, feature-flagging, or A/B testing platform experience—the core of this role
Limited evidence of telemetry/metrics infrastructure and statistical/experimental analysis (low data aptitude signal)
Career level and scope appear below Principal/IC6; no clear multi-workstream, cross-product technical leadership
